_about: Pinned latency baseline for NFR-003 of mission shared-package-boundary-cutover-01KQ22DS. The CI gate compares the median of N fresh `spec-kitty next --agent test --mission clean-install-fixture-01KQ22XX --json` runs against this number and fails the build if regression > tolerance_pct.
pre_cutover_methodology: 5 sequential subprocess runs on macOS Darwin 24.6.0, Python 3.14.0, editable-installed CLI in a fresh venv against the bundled clean-install fixture; median of wall-clock per `time.perf_counter()`.
_ci_target_note: CI runs a Python 3.13 clean wheel on GitHub-hosted Ubuntu, while pre_cutover_median_seconds was captured on macOS Python 3.14 editable install. The gate enforces current_median <= ci_target_median_seconds for CI stability; tolerance_pct remains the default for ad-hoc/local comparisons without this field. Recalibrated on 2026-06-02 from 1.000s to 1.050s after required CI measured a 1.019s median, a 19ms overshoot of the prior absolute ceiling.
_known_regression_note: TODO(Priivacy-ai/spec-kitty#2749): as of 2026-07-17 the gate is FLAKY at this ceiling boundary — with import-neutral changes it measured ~1.25s (FAIL) on one CI run and passed (<1.05s) on the next. Real latency increase from the doctrine-as-SSOT / step-authority work (#2723 step.yaml single source + step_projection runtime seam on every `next`; #2722) sits right on the 1.05s ceiling, so runner variance decides pass/fail. NOT caused by unrelated PRs. Do NOT recalibrate ci_target_median_seconds to mask it from within an unrelated change; the real fix is optimizing/caching step projection or a deliberate documented recalibration once #2749 is adjudicated.
